description: PAGE EIGHT Aircraft Continued from Page 1 298000000 in the first quarter of 1957 T As in the annual report he pre dieted that profits for 1958 would be slightly lower than in 1957 although still very substantial He said the drop in profits would result from the engineering ex penses on new products and the stopping of production of some piston engine models which were profitable because of the com panys long experience in making them Nuclear Engines Dismissed Asked about nuclear airplane engines Mr Horner noted that the government had cancelled its plans for an actual nuclear en 1 gine and now was only financing research by United Aircraft Whether it will result in any thing of a material interest from a production standpoint is a question I am not able to an swer he said Mr Horner said the govern ment had had a blow hot blow bold policy on nuclear aircraft engines One day it was a double X crash the next day it was a lets cancel he said The stockholders approved a unique supplemental retirement n plan for all employees It pro vides for a special fund for each employee in the firms pension
